key_insight: The paper formalizes core $q$-series machinery in Lean and uses it to verify landmark identities such as the Jacobi Triple Product and Rogers-Ramanujan identities.
engineering_relevance: It provides a reusable theorem-proving foundation for mechanically verified symbolic mathematics, supporting future formalization of advanced objects in number theory and mathematical physics.
method: Builds scalable Lean structures for $q$-Pochhammer symbols and $q$-binomial coefficients
Formalizes Bailey's Lemma and related $q$-series primitives
Uses the framework to prove classical identities end-to-end
evidence_signal: The abstract reports fully verified proofs of the Jacobi Triple Product formula and the Rogers-Ramanujan identities as demonstration benchmarks.
watch_item: The main implementation challenge is reconciling formal algebraic identities with analytic convergence requirements, which may complicate extension to broader classes of series.
paper_title: Formalized $q$-series: The Rogers-Ramanujan Identities and Beyond
